evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Ensure data quality through testing frameworks and monitoring
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals (testing frameworks, monitoring, CI/CD, data quality) dominate with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Intuitive is a global leader in robotic-assisted surgery, known for technologies like the da Vinci surgical system and Ion.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Intuitive is a regulated medical device company; while this specific role is supply chain/operations analytics rather than clinical, the regulated healthcare sector defaults to high domain risk.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Integrate applications with Snowflake, S3, Smartsheet, and REST APIs
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: An established Snowflake/dbt platform, enterprise data governance mentions, and a mentoring structure for junior engineers indicate a mature, specialised data organisation.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design and develop user-centric, interactive web applications using open-source frameworks such as Plotly Dash, Panel, or FastAPI
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Highly specific frameworks and platforms (Plotly Dash, Panel, FastAPI, Snowflake, S3, Smartsheet, Docker) give a very concrete picture of daily work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Partner with supply chain, operations, and analytics teams
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role centres on building internal data products and self-service analytics applications for supply chain/operations/analytics teams, an internal_data pattern.
evidence.autonomy_level: Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines using Python, dbt, Airflow, and/or Spark
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Designing and mentoring is ownership-oriented, but the role is also framed as partnering with and supporting supply chain/operations teams, giving mixed autonomy.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool or AI infrastructure mention appears anywhere in the extracted JD text.
evidence.testing_framing: Ensure data quality through testing frameworks and monitoring
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality is framed as an owned responsibility through testing frameworks and monitoring, with an explicit ownership verb.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Ensure data quality through testing frameworks and monitoring
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data quality and testing are a stated concern, but the JD does not raise explicit regulatory or compliance framing despite the regulated sector.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines using Python, dbt, Airflow, and/or Spark
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role mixes designing new data-driven applications with maintaining existing pipelines and platform integrations.
evidence.loss_aversion: Ensure data quality through testing frameworks and monitoring — reflects concern for data reliability rather than explicit regulatory compliance risk in this specific role.
